records.ety: [OE. camerike, fr. Cambrai (Flemish Kamerik), a city of France (formerly of Flanders), where it was first made.]

defs: A fine, thin, and white fabric made of flax or linen.
quotes: He hath ribbons of all the colors i' the rainbow; . . . inkles, caddises, cambrics, lawns.

defs: A fabric made, in imitation of linen cambric, of fine, hardspun cotton, often with figures of various colors; -- also called cotton cambric, and cambric muslin.
